results.possibleIdentity: Nathan B. Levine was a materials scientist and chemical engineer active in the 1960s and 1970s, based in Colonia, New Jersey. He spent much of his career at the Thiokol Chemical Corporation's Reaction Motors Division in Denville, New Jersey, where he specialized in high-performance polymers and elastomers for aerospace and defense applications. His research, often supported by NASA and the U.S. Air Force, focused on developing thermally stable and chemically resistant materials, such as nitroso rubbers and perhaloalkylene polymers, capable of withstanding corrosive rocket propellants like nitrogen tetroxide and hydrazine-type fuels. His technical expertise spanned fields including chemistry, adhesives, plastics, acrylics, foam, and fiberglass. Given his location and specialized knowledge in industrial materials, he was likely associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which maintained a registry of engineers and scientists to collaborate with artists on projects requiring expertise in advanced materials like foams and plastics.
